rulings.Case Title: SAMUEL GARCIA VS. LEVEL 8 CONSTRUCTION INC A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 15, 2010, line 1. CROSS COMPLAINANT BLAS ARTURO GARCIA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT denied. Motion for summary adjudication granted as to contribution cause of action as a matter of law. Parties agree that Level 8/Wong has not paid anything on the obligation. (Coca-Cola Bottling Company v. Lucky Stores, Inc. (1992) 11 Cal. App. 4th 1372, 13 Witkin, Summary of California Law, Equity, section 179.) Summary adjudication denied as to indemnity cause of action because there is a triable issue of fact regarding the scope of Blas Garcia's work on the project. (Wong declaration, paragraphs 6, 8 and 13; plaintiff's deposition 94:18-23 and 103:15-18.) Summary adjudication denied as to declaratory relief. (Filarsky v. Superior Court (2002) 28 Cal. 4th 419, 433.) =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM THOMAS VS. KENNETH ROBERSON et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 15, 2010, line 6. DEFENDANT MARY PIEL, M.D.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Off calendar as moot in view of ruling on special motion to strike. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM THOMAS VS. KENNETH ROBERSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike By Deft
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 15, 2010, line 6. DEFENDANT MARY PIEL, M.D.'S Special Motion To Strike. Granted. Plaintiff claim against defendant Piel arises from protected activity under CCP section 425.16. Defendant's letter to the Court is constitutionally protected speech under CCP section 425.16(e). Plaintiff fails to show he would prevail at trial. A duty of care does not extend to plaintiff. Defendant Piel's letter to the Family Law Court is absolutely privileged under CC section 47(b). Defendant is awarded reasonable attorney fees in the amount of $3219 and $490 in costs as the prevailing party. =(302/CWW)
